source: generated full-random (seed 14123330213291275571, seed_index 0, max-boundaries 384) DST trace at generator lash-sim.generated-workload.v8 on 2026-06-29; surfaced by the broad/full generated lane
classification: open cross-backend divergence; product-vs-harness under investigation; surfaced by the broad/full generated lane; not caught by lib-scale tests
discovered_by: lash-sim broad/full generated lane (this run), confirmed after the provider-event liveness fix
divergence_summary: After an active-turn queued-input cancel on turn 1 (the cancel succeeds), the lash SQLite store re-executes a subsequent turn on the same session with an extra provider exchange and yields EMPTY assistant output, while the in-memory ModelStore produces the correct output. The serial SQLite replay additionally DEADLOCKS when an active-turn queued-input enqueue blocks on a gated turn the serial replayer cannot advance.
model_replay: passes (this trace is model-replayable; the divergence is NOT a sim-harness/model gate issue)
sqlite_replay: KNOWN-DIVERGENT and DEADLOCK-PRONE — DO NOT run in CI; quarantined in KNOWN_SQLITE_DIVERGENCES (see crates/lash-sim/src/runner.rs). The serial SQLite replay hangs on an active-turn enqueue and otherwise diverges (empty assistant output / extra provider exchange).
minimization: not applied — the divergence is SCALE-dependent (does not reproduce at fast-random/lib scale), and the model-replay minimizer preserves only model behavior, so it does not preserve the SQLite divergence. The full broad-lane trace is the smallest verified repro available this pass.
scale_evidence: 491 events, 4 sessions, each with an active-turn queued-input cancel followed by subsequent provider turns (e.g. session-003 has 7 provider outputs after its cancel).
sqlite_replay_command_DO_NOT_RUN: cargo run -p lash-sim --locked -- replay-sqlite crates/lash-sim/replays/cross-backend-sqlite-active-turn-divergence/trace.json --out /tmp/lash-sim-divergence (DEADLOCKS / diverges — quarantined)
escalation: product-vs-harness decision pending; per the iteration-6 directive this divergence is documented as a discovered finding and NOT resolved this pass (no product patch).
